summary: Aspiration pneumonia is predominantly caused by bacterial pathogens rather than fungi, which only account for less than 5-15%. The majority of reported cases involve Gram-negative bacilli and aerobe gram-positive bacteria, making options suggesting otherwise incorrect. Fungi may occasionally be involved but rarely dominate causation significantly.
